title: Functions, Callbacks, Context, and Memory Interfaces · Memory Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted typed callback and context registry, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in typed callback and context registry and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.
Bind callback, context pointer, ownership, thread, and unregister lifetime into one interface contract.
Inject matched allocate, resize, and release operations with context and failure semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Function Pointer Compatibility
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug native-systems team handles memory case 5338. Produce bounded C evidence to bind callback results to explicit context lifetime; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Declare and call function pointers with exactly compatible prototypes and return conventions.

Inspect the Function Pointer Compatibility trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Function Pointer Compatibility.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Function Pointer Compatibility, keep Function Pointer Compatibility connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.
